title: spergula arvensis subsp. vulgaris
intro: Spergula arvensis subsp. vulgaris, commonly known as corn spurry, is a perennial or biennial weed native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat conditions such as kidney stones and arthritis. The health benefits of corn spurry include its ability to reduce blood pressure, alleviate symptoms of menopause, and provide antioxidant properties. Corn spurry is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
